items.title: Lloyd Blankfein Misses Being Goldman Sachs CEO—Mostly When There’s a Market Crisis
items.description: He discusses his upcoming memoir, surviving office setbacks and the advice he gives Goldman partners to this day

items.title: Could This $14 Stock Be Your Ticket to Millionaire Status?
items.author: Steven Porrello
items.description: NuScale Power, a nuclear technology company with the only U.S. NRC-approved small modular reactor design, faces challenges despite significant long-term potential. While data center power demand could surge 175% by 2030, NuScale has no firm sales yet and is burning cash with losses. Trading at 68x sales with a $4.1B market cap, the stock presents high risk and volatility despite transformative technology potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company has significant technological advantages (only U.S. NRC-approved SMR design) and massive market opportunity (175% data center power demand growth by 2030), but faces near-term headwinds including no firm sales, cash burn, operating losses, and expensive valuation (68x sales). High risk-reward profile warrants cautious stance.
Mentioned only as source of research data on data center power demand projections; no direct investment thesis or company-specific information provided.
